verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (BiP/GRP78) is canonically ER-luminal, but has documented surface display in cancer cells and cell-death contexts. Cancer-cell ecto-BiP is a validated immunotherapy target; apoptotic/necroptotic cells externalize BiP to the PM. These non-baseline surface pools qualify as cell_state_induced. Baseline localization is ER-lumen (not surface-accessible), but the documented cancer and cell-death ecto-pools establish contextual surface accessibility.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Uncertain whether the cancer-cell and apoptotic ecto-pools are sufficiently large/stable to drive clinical surface-targeting programs, or whether they remain a minor fraction in most contexts. BiP is robustly ER-resident at baseline; the surface pool is non-canonical but documented.
